title: Supervised learning in DNA neural networks
methods: NUPACK sequence design and secondary structure prediction

data_scope.classification_tasks: 3 digit pairs from MNIST (0 vs 1, 3 vs 4, 6 vs 7)
data_scope.training_patterns: 10 per class per task (selected via 'good teacher' strategy)
data_scope.test_patterns: 12 per class per task (k-means clustering, k=12)
data_scope.network_size_species: over 700 distinct molecular species per test tube
data_scope.unique_strands: more than 1200 unique DNA strands across learning and testing

core_conclusion: DNA molecules can autonomously carry out supervised learning in vitro: a 100-bit, 2-class DNA neural network (700+ molecular species) correctly classifies MNIST handwritten digits after training entirely from molecular examples. This is the first experimental demonstration of supervised learning in a molecular system, exhibiting four hallmarks — independence, integration, generality, and stability — without any silicon hardware.
why_it_matters: First molecular system to implement the training phase (not just inference) entirely in chemistry; demonstrates 100-dimensional real-world pattern classification, vastly exceeding prior records (~12 signals); opens paths to molecular therapeutics with adaptive memory of biomarker encounters; introduces fabrication methodology for 1200+ strand systems from unpurified oligos.
why_nature: Landmark first in a multi-decade molecular computing quest; cross-disciplinary impact spanning biochemistry, ML hardware, and synthetic biology; fully quantified performance metrics and open data at CaltechDATA enabling replication; transformative societal vision spanning biomedicine to programmable materials.
added_value_to_my_research: Reservoir-style molecular encoding -> online-updating spatiotemporal KDE for real-time mobility/pandemic sensing
Sparse-graph WTA topology -> hierarchical tournament decomposition for scalable multi-class spatial classification in GWR/MGWR
Phase-separated condensate partitioning -> spatially federated ML design with local-only training and global prior sharing (privacy-preserving health analytics)
NUPACK-prior error correction -> physics-informed regularization framework for graph learning where structural models penalize known-biased directions
Closed-loop adaptive DNA circuit -> online-updating R2SFCA with real-time mobile-phone/telehealth recalibration for live spatial accessibility surfaces

related.title: Energy-efficient multimodal zero-shot learning using in-memory reservoir computing
related.relation: reservoir computing as alternative to batch molecular training; hardware-level learning without digital computers

related.title: Resistive memory-based zero-shot liquid state machine for multimodal event data learning
related.relation: liquid-state machine (spiking reservoir) as physical computing analogue to DNA winner-take-all circuits

related.title: Deep learning incorporating biologically inspired neural dynamics and in-memory computing
related.relation: biologically plausible in-memory learning; parallels DNA system's departure from von-Neumann separation of memory and compute

related.title: Fully analogue reinforcement learning with memristors
related.relation: fully analogue hardware learning system; similar goal of eliminating digital computer from learning loop

related.title: Dynamic and scalable DNA-based information storage
related.relation: DNA as information storage medium; complementary to this paper's DNA as an active computational memory

related.title: Collective dynamics and long-range order in thermal neuristor networks
related.relation: emergent collective computation in physical (non-digital) substrate; analogous to DNA seesaw WTA dynamics

figures.caption: Concept and design of supervised learning in DNA neural networks — abstract training/testing process and seesaw circuit implementation
Characterization of key motifs — fluorescence kinetics and crosstalk evaluation for weight and learning gates
Pattern classification with activatable memories — MNIST digit pair classification using in silico-loaded weights
Learned weights — 10x10 fluorescence arrays showing digit shapes emerging from molecular learning across 3 digit pairs
Scalability of pattern classification using learned weights — performance vs. activated-to-total bit ratio from 4 to 100 bits
100-bit pattern classification using learned weights — 72 representative tests after 3 training processes; species count comparison
